note_data.title: Bank reconciliation statements
keyPoints: Bank reconciliation statements are needed due to reasons for differences between cash book and bank statement balances
The process involves updating the cash book first before preparing the bank reconciliation statement
After updating the cash book, prepare the bank reconciliation statement to reconcile remaining differences
The cash book needs to be updated for items that appear on the bank statement but not yet recorded in the cash book
Items affecting the cash book include bank charges, interest received, direct debits, and standing orders from the bank statement
The bank reconciliation statement shows items that explain the difference between the updated cash book balance and bank statement balance
Unpresented cheques are cheques written by the business but not yet cleared by the bank
Outstanding deposits are amounts paid into the bank but not yet shown on the bank statement
The final bank reconciliation statement should reconcile the cash book balance with the bank statement balance

howToAnswer.instruction: Create a full bank reconciliation statement showing all adjustments clearly with proper headings and calculations
Give the reason why the difference exists, e.g. 'This is because the cheque was issued but not yet presented to the bank'
Show the adjusted balance after adding/subtracting the reconciling items

modelAnswer.question: The cash book shows a bank balance of Rs.15,400 (debit). The bank statement shows a balance of Rs.12,800 (credit). Unpresented cheques total Rs.3,200 and outstanding deposits are Rs.600. Prepare a bank reconciliation statement.
modelAnswer.answer:
Bank Reconciliation Statement as at [date]

Balance as per bank statement: Rs.12,800
Add: Outstanding deposits: Rs.600
: Rs.13,400
Less: Unpresented cheques: Rs.3,200
Adjusted bank balance: Rs.10,200

Balance as per cash book: Rs.15,400
Adjusted cash book balance: Rs.15,400

Note: There appears to be an error of Rs.5,200 that needs investigation.
markBreakdown: Mark 2: Starting with bank statement balance Rs.12,800
Mark 5: Showing adjusted bank balance Rs.10,200
Mark 6: Comparing with cash book balance

commonMistakes: Not updating the cash book before preparing the bank reconciliation statement
Confusing items that should update the cash book versus items that appear on the bank reconciliation statement
Incorrectly handling unpresented cheques and outstanding deposits in the reconciliation process
examinerTips: Always update the cash book first before preparing the bank reconciliation statement
Remember that items known to the bank but not the business require cash book updates
formulas: Adjusted bank balance = Bank statement balance + Outstanding deposits - Unpresented cheques
Reconciliation check: Adjusted cash book balance = Adjusted bank statement balance
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Bank reconciliation statement format showing bank statement balance at top, adding outstanding deposits, subtracting unpresented cheques, and comparing final adjusted balance with cash book balance
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in O/N 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to advise whether Sinita should prepare bank reconciliation statements monthly rather than every three months, requiring two advantages and two disadvantages with justification
M/J 2022 P1 Q7 (8 marks) tested preparation of a complete bank reconciliation statement given cash book and bank statement extracts, including unpresented cheques and bank charges
O/N 2021 P2 Q3 (10 marks) required students to prepare a bank reconciliation and explain the purpose of bank reconciliation statements to business owners
M/J 2023 P1 Q6 (6 marks) focused on identifying timing differences and explaining why bank reconciliation is a form of internal control
O/N 2020 P2 Q5 (8 marks) tested correction of cash book errors identified through bank reconciliation process

workedExample.question: Prepare a bank reconciliation statement as at 31 March 2024. Cash book balance: $2,450 (debit). Bank statement balance: $1,890 (credit). Unpresented cheques: $380. Outstanding deposits: $720. Bank charges on statement not in cash book: $100. (8 marks)
steps: Step 1: Start with the corrected cash book balance: $2,450 - $100 bank charges = $2,350
Step 2: Create reconciliation statement starting with bank statement balance $1,890
Step 3: Add outstanding deposits (money paid in but not yet on statement): $1,890 + $720 = $2,610
Step 4: Subtract unpresented cheques (cheques written but not yet cleared): $2,610 - $380 = $2,230
Step 5: Note the difference indicates an error needs investigation: $2,350 - $2,230 = $120 difference
workedExample.answer: Bank Reconciliation Statement as at 31 March 2024: Balance per bank statement $1,890, Add: Outstanding deposits $720, Less: Unpresented cheques ($380), Adjusted bank balance $2,230. Note: Difference of $120 from corrected cash book balance requires investigation.
workedExample.markScheme: 2 marks for correct format/heading, 2 marks for outstanding deposits added correctly, 2 marks for unpresented cheques subtracted correctly, 1 mark for starting balance, 1 mark for final calculated balance

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused which items to add and subtract' — always remember outstanding deposits are added (increasing bank balance) while unpresented cheques are subtracted
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students often forgot to update the cash book first' — always correct cash book errors (like bank charges) before preparing the reconciliation statement
Examiner Report 2024: 'Candidates lost marks for poor presentation' — always use proper headings, show workings clearly, and state the final reconciled balance prominently
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw the standard bank reconciliation format: Start with 'Balance per bank statement $X', then show '+ Outstanding deposits $X', then '- Unpresented cheques $(X)', finally '= Adjusted bank balance $X'. Include proper heading with business name and date. Show a separate cash book correction section above if needed, listing items like bank charges, direct debits, or interest that appear on bank statement but not in cash book.
note_data.quickRecap: Bank reconciliation statements are prepared to explain differences between cash book and bank statement balances. The process involves first updating the cash book for items shown on the bank statement but not yet recorded. Then prepare the bank reconciliation statement to account for timing differences like unpresented cheques and outstanding deposits.
